deck: Part three of Dimagi's 20th anniversary series looks back at 2010 to 2014, when two USAID Development Innovation Ventures grants funded dozens of CommCare pilots across India and pushed the team's learning velocity to its peak.
seo_description: How USAID DIV funding drove dozens of CommCare pilots in India from 2010 to 2014, and the learnings on motivation, training, and why failure is never free.
og_description: Inside Dimagi's USAID DIV era: dozens of CommCare pilots in India and the hard-won lesson that failure is never free.

pull_quote.text: Unlike other technology sectors, the cost of failure is extremely high, but it's not born by the organization. It's born by the user. So I think we do need to have that mindset, but also realize you can't just fail for free.

bullets: The DIV mechanism: a stage one grant to equip 10 organizations, then a stage two scale-up to 40 organizations across India
The CRS proof point: a Catholic Relief Services maternal and child health project that scaled from 10 pilot users to 285 frontline workers
Built with the user's voice, literally, with a community health worker recorded for the app's multimedia counseling on J2ME brick phones
Intrinsic vs extrinsic motivation: research on feeding performance data back to health workers in ways that motivate rather than shame
Failure is not free: the real cost to users when a project equips them, then drops support, and why adoption is a funnel with a human price
Over-invest in training: teaching digital literacy and Hindi typing from scratch, and treating reusable training collateral as core to scale
Practices that evolve: weekly field reports and ad hoc communication that fit a small team but had to change on the road to national scale

stats.label: organizations across India supported in the stage two DIV grant
frontline workers the CRS project scaled to, from an initial 10
social return on investment DIV measured for CommCare

guests.name: Kriti Mehrotra
guests.role: Impact Advisor in the Office of the CEO, who joined as a field manager and trained early CommCare users in rural India.

guests.name: Jeremy Wacksman
guests.role: Global Director of Division Operations, a former field fellow who led the CRS motivation research on the ground.

guests.name: Sri Ranganathan
guests.role: Director of Customer Success, who managed the CRS deployment and helped shape Dimagi's deployment playbook.
